name: Hollandaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Into a heatproof bowl, whisk together egg yolks, lemon juice, salt, sugar, dry mustard, and Tabasco sauce.
2. Place about one inch of water in the bottom of a double boiler. Bring to a simmer over low heat.
3. Put the top part of the double boiler over the simmering water.
4. Add 1 stick (½ cup) of very cold butter to the egg yolk mixture, whisking constantly until the sauce thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
5. Keep an eye on the sides and bottom to prevent boiling over.
6. Once thickened, remove from heat and serve immediately. If it curdles later, add ½ teaspoon of milk and whisk vigorously.
This recipe is known for not curdling if made correctly. If leftovers are reheated and curdle, adding a small amount of milk (about ½ to 1 teaspoon) and whisking helps restore the sauce.
